question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take] Can an unprivileged taker, using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where the loan token silently deducts a transfer fee, trigger take() -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, sellerAssets), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] must not exceed actual token balance received, causing scoped impact: systematic value leakage where claimableSettlementFee accumulates phantom fees never backed by real tokens? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer ERC20 as loanToken, execute take() with nonzero settlement fee, assert claimableSettlementFee[token] > (balanceOf(midnight, token) - pre_balance).

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower, using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger repay() -> position[id][onBehalf].debt -= units -> marketState[id].withdrawable += units -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), units), violating the invariant that withdrawable must not exceed actual token balance held, causing scoped impact: insolvency where lenders withdraw more tokens than the contract holds? Proof idea: fuzz fee rate and repay units, assert sum(withdrawable) <= balanceOf(midnight, loanToken) after repay.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower, using a fee-on-transfer collateral token, under preconditions where the collateral token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger supplyCollateral() -> position[id][onBehalf].collateral[index] += assets -> safeTransferFrom(collateralToken, msg.sender, address(this), assets), violating the invariant that recorded collateral must not exceed actual token balance, causing scoped impact: bad debt creation where borrower borrows against phantom collateral? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer collateral, supply assets, borrow max, assert isHealthy returns false when oracle prices are correct.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, using a fee-on-transfer loan token, under preconditions where the loan token deducts a fee on transferFrom, trigger liquidate() -> _position.debt -= repaidUnits -> marketState[id].withdrawable += repaidUnits -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits), violating the invariant that withdrawable must be backed by actual token balance, causing scoped impact: insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw because withdrawable exceeds real balance? Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits and fee rate, assert withdrawable <= balanceOf(midnight, loanToken) post-liquidation.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransfer / safeTransferFrom / flashLoan] Can an unprivileged flash loan caller, using a fee-on-transfer token, under preconditions where the token deducts a fee on both transfer and transferFrom, trigger flashLoan() -> safeTransfer(token, callback, assets[i]) -> onFlashLoan callback -> safeTransferFrom(token, callback, address(this), assets[i]), violating the invariant that the protocol's token balance must not decrease after a flash loan, causing scoped impact: direct loss of fee amount per flash loan call? Proof idea: deploy fee-on-transfer token, call flashLoan, assert balanceOf(midnight, token) decreases by fee * assets.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (buy offer, dust units)] Can an unprivileged taker, acting as seller on a buy offer with dust units, under preconditions where units=1 and buyerPrice is very small (e.g., 1 wei), trigger take() -> buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0 -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), 0) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, 0), violating the invariant that every credit unit increase must correspond to a nonzero asset transfer, causing scoped impact: extractable value where buyer receives 1 credit unit for 0 loan tokens paid? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD-1] and buyerPrice in [1, WAD-1], assert buyerAssets > 0 whenever buyerCreditIncrease > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (sell offer, dust units)] Can an unprivileged maker posting a sell offer, under preconditions where units=1 and sellerPrice rounds mulDivUp(1, sellerPrice, WAD) to 0 (impossible since mulDivUp(1,x,WAD)>=1 for x>0, but sellerPrice=0 when settlementFee=offerPrice), trigger take() -> sellerAssets = mulDivUp(1, 0, WAD) = 0 -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, receiver, 0), violating the invariant that a sell offer must transfer nonzero assets to the seller when debt increases, causing scoped impact: seller incurs debt obligation for 0 received assets? Proof idea: set settlementFee = offerPrice (fee equals full price), take sell offer with units=1, assert sellerAssets == 0 and sellerDebtIncrease == 1.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransferFrom / take (spread rounding to zero)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where settlementFee is nonzero but units * settlementFee < WAD, trigger take() -> buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0 -> claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += 0 -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), 0), violating the invariant that a nonzero settlement fee must always result in nonzero fee collection, causing scoped impact: systematic fee leakage where protocol collects zero fee despite nonzero fee rate? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD/settlementFee - 1], assert claimableSettlementFee increases by at least 1 when settlementFee > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: settlementFee / safeTransferFrom / take (TTM interpolation truncation)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where timeToMaturity falls between two breakpoints with feeLower < feeUpper and the interpolation formula (feeLower * (end - ttm) + feeUpper * (ttm - start)) / (end - start) truncates to a value less than feeLower, trigger take() with an artificially low computed settlementFee, violating the invariant that the settlement fee must be monotonically bounded between adjacent breakpoints, causing scoped impact: extractable value where taker pays less spread than the fee schedule mandates? Proof idea: differential test settlementFee(id, ttm) against a reference implementation using full-precision arithmetic for all ttm in [0, 360 days].

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: settlementFee / safeTransferFrom / take (TTM = maturity boundary)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where block.timestamp == market.maturity (timeToMaturity = 0 via zeroFloorSub), trigger take() -> settlementFee(id, 0) = settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP -> potentially 0 if cbp0 = 0, violating the invariant that post-maturity fee must equal the 0-day breakpoint fee, causing scoped impact: zero-fee trades at maturity boundary allowing takers to extract value from the spread? Proof idea: set cbp0 = 0, warp to maturity, take offer, assert claimableSettlementFee does not increase despite nonzero cbp1..cbp6.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: settlementFee / safeTransferFrom / take (TTM breakpoint discontinuity)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where timeToMaturity is exactly at a breakpoint boundary (e.g., 1 day, 7 days, 30 days), trigger take() with a settlement fee that differs discontinuously from the fee one second earlier, violating the invariant that the fee function must be continuous and monotone between breakpoints, causing scoped impact: extractable value by timing trades to exploit fee jumps at breakpoint boundaries? Proof idea: compare settlementFee(id, breakpoint) vs settlementFee(id, breakpoint + 1) for all 7 breakpoints, assert no discontinuity exceeds 1 CBP.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: settlementFee / safeTransferFrom / take (TTM >= 360 days flat cap)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where timeToMaturity >= 360 days and settlementFeeCbp6 = 0, trigger take() -> settlementFee returns 0 -> spread = 0 -> claimableSettlementFee unchanged, violating the invariant that long-dated markets must collect nonzero fees, causing scoped impact: systematic fee leakage for all trades in markets with maturity > 360 days from now? Proof idea: create market with maturity = now + 361 days, set cbp6 = 0, take offer, assert fee collected is 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / mulDivUp / take (overflow in buyerAssets)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where units is near type(uint128).max and buyerPrice is near WAD, trigger take() -> buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) where units * buyerPrice overflows uint256, causing an arithmetic revert or silent wrap-around, violating the invariant that take must not revert for valid market parameters, causing scoped impact: DoS of take for large but valid unit amounts? Proof idea: fuzz units in [WAD+1, type(uint128).max] and buyerPrice in [WAD/2, WAD], assert take does not revert with arithmetic overflow.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivUp / take (overflow in sellerAssets for sell offer)] Can an unprivileged taker taking a sell offer, under preconditions where units is large and buyerPrice is near WAD, trigger take() -> sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) where units * buyerPrice + (WAD - 1) overflows uint256, causing arithmetic revert, violating the invariant that sell offer takes must succeed for valid parameters, causing scoped impact: DoS of sell offer settlement for large unit amounts? Proof idea: fuzz units near type(uint256).max / WAD and buyerPrice near WAD, assert mulDivUp does not overflow.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / take (continuousFee * timeToMaturity overflow)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where continuousFee is at MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE (~317097919) and timeToMaturity is near 100 years (~3.15e9 seconds), trigger take() -> buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) where continuousFee * timeToMaturity overflows uint256 before the mulDivDown, causing arithmetic revert or incorrect pendingFee, violating the invariant that pendingFee must accurately reflect the continuous fee obligation, causing scoped impact: DoS of take or incorrect fee crystallization? Proof idea: set continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, create market with maturity = now + 100 years, take offer, assert no overflow.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / take (buyerPendingFeeIncrease toUint128 overflow)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where buyerCreditIncrease is near type(uint128).max and continuousFee * timeToMaturity / WAD > 1, trigger take() -> buyerPendingFeeIncrease = toUint128(buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD)) where the result exceeds type(uint128).max, causing CastOverflow revert, violating the invariant that take must not revert for valid credit increases, causing scoped impact: DoS of large credit-increasing takes in high-fee long-dated markets? Proof idea: fuzz buyerCreditIncrease, continuousFee, timeToMaturity, assert toUint128 does not revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivUp / take (sellerPendingFeeDecrease overflow)] Can an unprivileged taker, under preconditions where sellerPos.pendingFee is near type(uint128).max and sellerCreditDecrease equals sellerPos.credit, trigger take() -> sellerPendingFeeDecrease = toUint128(sellerPos.pendingFee.mulDivUp(sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit)) where mulDivUp(pendingFee, credit, credit) = pendingFee + 0 = pendingFee (no overflow), but with sellerCreditDecrease > sellerPos.credit (impossible by min), verify the min() guard is tight, violating the invariant that pendingFeeDecrease must not exceed pendingFee, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption if guard is insufficient? Proof idea: invariant test asserting sellerPendingFeeDecrease <= sellerPos.pendingFee for all fuzzed inputs.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / liquidate (post-maturity LIF integer truncation)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where block.timestamp = market.maturity + 1 (one second after maturity), trigger liquidate(postMaturityMode=true) -> lif = WAD + (maxLif - WAD) * 1 / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF where TIME_TO_MAX_LIF = 900 and (maxLif - WAD) < 900, causing lif = WAD (no incentive), violating the invariant that post-maturity liquidation must provide nonzero incentive to liquidators, causing scoped impact: liquidation unprofitability at maturity+1 creating a window where unhealthy positions cannot be economically liquidated? Proof idea: set maxLif = WAD + 899, warp to maturity + 1, assert lif > WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / liquidate (seizedAssets double truncation)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where repaidUnits is small and lif is near WAD, trigger liquidate() -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice) where double truncation causes seizedAssets = 0 despite repaidUnits > 0, violating the invariant that a nonzero repay must seize nonzero collateral, causing scoped impact: liquidator repays debt but receives 0 collateral, creating a loss for the liquidator and potential bad debt? Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits in [1, 1000], lif near WAD, high liquidatedCollatPrice, assert seizedAssets > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivUp / liquidate (repaidUnits double round-up)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where seizedAssets is small and liquidatedCollatPrice is large, trigger liquidate() -> repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) where double round-up causes repaidUnits to exceed the fair value of seized collateral, violating the invariant that liquidation profit must be bounded by LIF, causing scoped impact: liquidator repays more than LIF * collateral_value, creating bad debt for lenders? Proof idea: fuzz seizedAssets in [1, 1000] and liquidatedCollatPrice, assert repaidUnits * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE <= seizedAssets * liquidatedCollatPrice * maxLif / WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivUp / liquidate (maxRepaid overflow)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where (debt - maxDebt) is large and lif * lltv is close to WAD * WAD, trigger liquidate() -> maxRepaid = (debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) where WAD * WAD = 1e36 and (debt - maxDebt) * 1e36 overflows uint256, causing arithmetic revert, violating the invariant that liquidation must not revert for valid unhealthy positions, causing scoped impact: DoS of liquidation for deeply unhealthy positions with high LIF and high LLTV? Proof idea: fuzz debt - maxDebt near type(uint128).max and lif * lltv near WAD * WAD, assert no overflow.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / updatePositionView (continuous fee accrual truncation)] Can an unprivileged lender, under preconditions where postSlashPendingFee is small and (accrualEnd - lastAccrual) / (maturity - lastAccrual) is a small fraction, trigger updatePositionView() -> fee = postSlashPendingFee.mulDivDown(accrualEnd - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual) = 0 despite nonzero pendingFee and elapsed time, violating the invariant that continuous fee must accrue proportionally over time, causing scoped impact: systematic fee leakage where lenders avoid paying continuous fees by interacting frequently in small increments? Proof idea: fuzz lastAccrual, maturity, pendingFee, assert fee > 0 whenever pendingFee > 0 and accrualEnd > lastAccrual.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivUp / withdraw (pendingFeeDecrease over-deduction)] Can an unprivileged lender, under preconditions where position.credit is 1 and pendingFee is near type(uint128).max, trigger withdraw() -> pendingFeeDecrease = toUint128(pendingFee.mulDivUp(units, credit)) where mulDivUp(pendingFee, units, 1) = pendingFee * units overflows uint256, causing arithmetic revert, violating the invariant that withdraw must not revert for valid credit amounts, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where lender cannot withdraw their credit? Proof idea: set credit = 1, pendingFee = type(uint128).max, units = 1, assert withdraw does not revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: mulDivDown / lossFactor (bad debt precision loss with tiny totalUnits)] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under preconditions where totalUnits is very small (e.g., 1) and badDebt equals totalUnits - 1, trigger liquidate() -> lossFactor = type(uint128).max - (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(totalUnits - badDebt, totalUnits) where mulDivDown(x, 1, 1) = x but mulDivDown(x, 0, 1) = 0 causing lossFactor = type(uint128).max, violating the invariant that lossFactor must not reach type(uint128).max unless all credit is lost, causing scoped impact: take reverts with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut for all subsequent trades? Proof idea: create market with 1 unit of credit, realize bad debt of 0 units, assert lossFactor < type(uint128).max.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: safeTransfer / withdrawCollateral (ERC777 reentrancy)] Can an unprivileged borrower, using an ERC777 collateral token with a tokensReceived hook, under preconditions where the borrower is near the health threshold, trigger withdrawCollateral() -> isHealthy check passes -> safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) -> ERC777 hook reenters withdrawCollateral() -> second isHealthy check uses already-reduced collateral, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must leave the position healthy, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure where borrower withdraws more collateral than health allows? Proof idea: implement ERC777 hook that calls withdrawCollateral again, assert final position is healthy.
